title: -Grant Agreement Amendment with State Coastal Conservancy to reallocate $1.7M to a) the Bay Trail Segment at Shorebird Park (design phase) and b) recreation improvements at the Gateway Pier and the J&K Parking Lot

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a grant agreement amendment
with the State Coastal Conservancy (SCC) to re-allocate $1.7M in unspent funds from the
Cesar Chavez Park Perimeter Trail Project to a) the Bay Trail Segment at Shorebird Park
(design phase) and b) recreation improvements at the Gateway Pier and the J&K Parking
Lot.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
In 2022, the City allocated $2M of the $15M State Coastal Conservancy FY2022 surplus
funds grant for the design and construction of the Cesar Chavez Perimeter Pathway. Due
to ongoing environmental monitoring and compliance activities at Cesar Chavez Park, the
Cesar Chavez Park Perimeter Pathway Project is paused at the design phase. The
remaining project funds in the amount of $1.7M will be re-allocated by a grant amendment
to a) the Bay Trail Segment at Shorebird Park (design phase) and b) recreation
improvements at the Gateway Pier and the J&K Parking Lot.
sections.current_situation:
In FY2022, the State of California allocated a one-time distribution of state surplus funds to
cities and counties throughout California. Berkeley received a one-time allocation of $15M
for Berkeley Waterfront improvement projects and the State Coastal Conservancy (SCC)
was selected to administer these funds. On December 13, 2022, the City Council approved
the execution of five grant contracts with SCC for five improvement projects at the Berkeley
Waterfront and began the design and construction phases (Resolution No. 70,629-N.S.)
The five projects included: 1) Dredging of the Marina Main Channel (completion by
December 2025); 2) Replacement of D and E Dock system (completion in early 2026); 3)
Improvements to Cesar Chavez Park Pathways (design completed); 4) Improvements to the
South Cove West Parking Lot (completion by December 2025); and 5) Environmental
Review and Design of the Pier-Ferry Project (completion in 2027).

Grant contract with SCC to re-allocate $1.7M from the Cesar Chavez Park Restroom to CONSENT CALENDAR
the Bay Trail / Gateway Pier / J&K Lot Projects September 9, 2025
In the spring of 2025, the Cesar Chavez Park Perimeter Pathway Project was paused at the
design phase due to ongoing environmental monitoring and compliance activities, leaving
$1.7M in remaining SCC funds. In order to identify a new project that can be completed
within the grant deadline (2029), City and SCC staff identified the following two related
projects that enhance Bay Trail connectivity that can be completed with this re-allocated
funding: a) the Bay Trail Segment at Shorebird Park (design phase), and b) recreation
improvements at the Gateway Pier and the J&K Parking Lot.

sections.background:
In the past 15 years, Council has funded over $40.2 million in important improvement
projects at the Berkeley Waterfront.1 The following projects have been completed or are
currently in-process:
Current and Recently Completed Construction Projects
 South Cove East Parking Lot and Restroom and Bay Trail Project – Completed.
 South Cove Dock Float Pile Replacement Project – Completed.
 South Cove East and West Docks Replacement Project – Completed.
 Marina Streets Project (University Ave / Marina Blvd / Spinnaker Way) – Completed.
 Finger Dock Replacement, Phases 1-4 – Completed.
1 See Off Agenda Memo to Council on February 16, 2022: https://berkeleyca.gov/sites/default/files/2022-
04/Waterfront%20CIP%20Projects%20021622.pdf
Page 2
Page 3 of 4
Grant contract with SCC to re-allocate $1.7M from the Cesar Chavez Park Restroom to CONSENT CALENDAR
the Bay Trail / Gateway Pier / J&K Lot Projects September 9, 2025
 Piling Replacement Project – Completed.
 O and K Dock Electrical System Renovation – currently in construction.
 K Dock Restroom Renovation – currently in construction.
 Dredging of Marina Main Channel and Inner Harbor – currently in construction.
 South Cove West Parking Lot – currently construction.
 South Cove East Dock Pier Pile Repair – currently in construction.
 South Cove Middle Dock ADA Float Replacement – currently in construction.
 D&E Dock Replacement Project – currently in construction.
 N Dock Parking Lot Renovation – in-process.
Current Planning-only Projects
 Cesar Chavez Park Restroom – in-process.
 Cesar Chavez Perimeter Trail – design completed.
 Dredging of the South Sailing Basin – in-process.
 Waterfront Specific Plan – in-process.
 Pier-Ferry Project – in-process.
 South Cove Seawall Replacement – in-process.
 Bay Trail - Marina Blvd Segment – in-process.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The Berkeley Waterfront improvement projects identified in this report will help the City to

sections.rationale:
In the spring of 2025, due to ongoing environmental monitoring and compliance activities at
Cesar Chavez Park, the Cesar Chavez Park Perimeter Pathway Project was paused,
leaving $1.7M in remaining SCC funds. Working with SCC staff, the City has identified two
related projects in need of funding that enhance Bay Trail connectivity: a) the Bay Trail
Segment at Shorebird Park and b) recreation improvements at the Gateway Pier and the
J&K Parking Lot and associated Inner Harbor Pathway.

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
GRANT AGREEMENT AMENDMENT WITH STATE COASTAL CONSERVANCY TO RE-
ALLOCATE $1.7M TO THE BAY TRAIL SEGMENT AT SHOREBIRD PARK AND
RECREATION IMPROVEMENTS AT THE GATEWAY PIER AND J&K PARKING LOT AT
THE BERKELEY WATERFRONT
WHEREAS, in FY2022, the State of California allocated a one-time distribution of state
surplus funds to cities and counties throughout California; and
WHEREAS, Berkeley received a one-time FY2022 state budget allocation of $15 million for
Berkeley Waterfront improvement projects; and
WHEREAS, the State Coastal Conservancy (SCC) was designated to administer these funds
via their grants and contracts program; and
WHEREAS, on December 13, 2022, the City Council approved the execution of five grant
contracts with SCC for five improvement projects and began the design and construction
phases (Resolution No. 70,629-N.S.); and
WHEREAS, in the spring of 2025, due to ongoing environmental monitoring and compliance
activities at Cesar Chavez Park, the Cesar Chavez Park Perimeter Pathway Project was
paused at the design phase, leaving $1.7M in unspent SCC funds; and
WHEREAS, working with SCC staff, the City has identified two related projects that enhance
Bay Trail connectivity that can be completed with this re-allocated funding: a) the Bay Trail
Segment at Shorebird Park (design phase) and b) recreation improvements at the Gateway
Pier and the J&K Parking Lot.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that $1.7M in
unspent SCC funds from the FY2022 $15M allocation to the Berkeley Waterfront will now be
re-allocated to a) the Bay Trail Segment at Shorebird Park (design phase) and b) recreation
improvements at the Gateway Pier and the J&K Parking Lot; and that the City Manager or
their designee is authorized to execute all grant submittal packages, grant contracts and
amendments, grant reimbursement submittals, and all other grant project-related documents
with the State Coastal
